inspectedFor386Selection.scopeDescription: The 35 pixels selected by the best FOR-386 local fringe discriminator, analyzed only as diagnostic input.

fringeDiscriminatorCandidates.description: Keeps the round useful lane at alpha >= 160 and the narrowest butt-bevel fringe lane at alpha >= 96.
Uses orthogonal coverage topology to keep transition pixels on the round lane and terminal edge pixels on the butt lane.
Tests whether the useful round lane separates by equal source/coverage alpha and the 160 alpha lane.
fringeDiscriminatorCandidates.selectionMethod: round-round && bandLocalX in 39..45 && coverageAlphaByte == transparentSourceAlphaByte && coverageAlphaByte >= 160

fringeDiscriminatorCandidates.description: Checks the butt-bevel portion of the residual set using edge distance and source/coverage alpha relation only.
Keeps alpha-160 fringe pixels that have at least one partial orthogonal neighbor.
Keeps the full useful lane while excluding only the farthest FOR-386 residual butt-bevel edge pixels.
Narrows the FOR-386 local fringe window by requiring proximity to the stroke-band edge.

bestFringeDiscriminatorCandidate.runtimeBlocker: FOR-387 is diagnostic-only; any candidate must be proven outside the FOR-386 residual audit scope before renderer use.

classificationReason: The best residual fringe discriminator keeps all 8 useful pixels, but still includes regressed pixels from the FOR-386 selection.
nextMove: Do not enable the correction. Export a renderer-owned source/coverage composition or fringe-topology signal finer than local band coordinates. Best candidate: local-window-edge-distance-le-17.
